lessons.title: 🚀 Hello Terminal!
lessons.content:
# Welcome to the Terminal!

The terminal (also called command line, shell, or console) is a way to talk directly to your computer using text commands.

Instead of clicking buttons, you TYPE what you want to do!

challenges.hint: Just type pwd and hit enter

challenges.task: Type `ls` to see what files are here

lessons.title: 📁 Navigating Files
lessons.content:
# Moving Around

Use `cd` (Change Directory) to move between folders!

- `cd foldername` - Go into a folder
- `cd ..` - Go back one level
- `cd ~` - Go home
- `cd -` - Go back to where you were

lessons.title: 📝 Creating Files & Folders
lessons.content:
# Making Things!

- `mkdir foldername` - Create a new folder
- `touch filename.txt` - Create a new empty file

Let's make some stuff!

lessons.title: 🔍 Finding Things
lessons.content:
# Searching

- `grep 'word' file` - Find lines containing 'word'
- `find . -name '*.txt'` - Find all .txt files

Search like a detective!

challenges.hint: Type exactly: echo 'Hello World' | grep World

lessons.title: 🔐 Permissions
lessons.content:
# Permissions

Files have permissions: who can read, write, or run them!

- `ls -l` - See permissions
- `chmod +x file` - Make a file runnable

Permissions: r (read) = 4, w (write) = 2, x (execute) = 1
